scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all chord tones (E, G#, B) plus safe extensions (F#, C#). Zero avoid notes. The most guitar-friendly major pentatonic scale.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Target the root (E) and major 3rd (G#) for resolution. Perfect for rock guitar in the open position.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The go-to scale for E major in rock, country, and blues. Extremely guitar-friendly. Ideal for memorable hooks and solos.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The fundamental E major scale with four sharps (F#, C#, G#, D#). Complete melodic freedom.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work over I major. The A (11th) can sound suspended - resolve to G# or B.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over E major. Essential for full diatonic expression. Works in all styles.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Raises A to A# (#11) creating dreamy, ethereal quality. Modern, floating major sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the #11 (A#) for signature Lydian color. Creates uplifting quality.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for Emaj7#11 or modern I major sounds. Perfect for film scores, jazz, and fusion.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Lowers D# to D (b7) creating dominant 7th character. Perfect for bluesy E major.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The D (b7) creates bluesy edge. Great for rock and blues guitar.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for E7 chords and blues-rock vamps. Essential for jam bands and folk rock.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Built on V - creates bluesy major sound
scaleCompatibility.rationale: All notes diatonic to E major. Classic rock guitar approach to E major chords.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Creates bluesy major without clash. Perfect for rock guitar soloing.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential rock guitar scale over E major. Safe and bluesy.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. D# is a chord tone (maj7).
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the maj7 (D#). All tones work beautifully.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Complete freedom over Emaj7. Perfect for jazz, pop, R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (A#) avoids suspended sound. THE scale for Imaj7 in modern jazz.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (A#) is signature sound. Essential for Emaj7#11 voicings.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive modern Imaj7 scale. Essential for jazz and fusion.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: F# Dorian is the natural ii mode in E major. Major 6th (D#) creates jazzier minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the D# (13th) for Dorian character. Essential for ii-V-I.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: The definitive ii chord scale. Essential for jazz, pop, R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Natural minor with D instead of D#. Traditional, somber minor sound.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The D (b13) creates darker color. More traditional than Dorian.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for darker ii chord sound. More melancholic approach.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. D# (13th) essential for ii-V-I.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: D# (13th) crucial for Dorian color. Smooth voice leading to B7.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for F#m7-B7-Emaj7. The standard iim7 sound.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: E major reinforces key center. All notes work since F#m7 is diatonic to E.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think E major, emphasize F# as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for key center emphasis. Great for teaching.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural iii mode with exotic b2
scaleCompatibility.rationale: G# Phrygian is iii mode of E major. b2 (A) creates Spanish/exotic character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize the b2 (A) for Phrygian color. Creates dark, mysterious quality.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for exotic, Spanish, or metal-influenced iii minor.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: All E major notes work over G#m. Maintains key center.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think E major, emphasize G# as root. Simple diatonic approach.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key center over iii.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: A major is the natural scale for IV in E major. Creates subdominant function.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize root (A) and major 3rd (C#). D (11th) can sound suspended.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for I-IV-V progressions. Perfect for rock, folk, and country.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: The #4 (D#) creates modern, floating IV sound. Common in jazz.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The #11 (D#) creates bright, dreamy quality. Perfect for Amaj7#11.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, modern IV sound. Perfect for jazz and fusion.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: E major over IV maintains key center. All notes work.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think E major, emphasize A as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for diatonic IV approach. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Natural V chord mode in E major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: B Mixolydian shares all notes with E major. b7 (A) creates dominant 7th character.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The A (b7) creates dominant pull to E. Emphasize D# and A for strong V-I.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for V7 chords in E major. Perfect for dominant-tonic resolution.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: B major provides pure V triad. A# (maj7) is brighter than Mixolydian's A.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: More classical V approach. A# creates major 7th rather than dominant 7th.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for classical, traditional V sound.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: E major contains all V chord tones. B Mixolydian = E Ionian starting on B.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think E major, emphasize B as root. A creates natural dominant character.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic V approach. Maintains key center.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones perfectly. b7 (A) is a chord tone.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Emphasize 3rd (D#) and b7 (A) - the tritone that resolves to E.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for all V7 situations. Perfect for ii-V-I (F#m7-B7-Emaj7).

scaleCompatibility.rationale: E major over B7 maintains key center. Simple and effective.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think E major, emphasize B as root. A creates dominant 7th automatically.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for maintaining key awareness over V7.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Adds blue notes for authentic blues dominant. b5 (F) creates blues tension.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Use F (b5) as passing tone to F#. Creates authentic blues sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for blues and blues-rock B7. Perfect for 12-bar blues in E.

scaleCompatibility.relationship: Relative minor - shares all notes with E major
scaleCompatibility.rationale: C# minor is vi of E major. Shares all seven notes. Most common minor chord in E major.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All E major notes work perfectly. Natural minor sound in the key.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vi chords in E major. Perfect for E-B-C#m-A progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Dorian adds A# (major 6th) for brighter minor. More sophisticated.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: The A# (major 6th) creates jazzy, bright minor. More modern than Aeolian.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Use for sophisticated, jazzy vi minor. Perfect for jazz, funk, R&B.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: E major and C# minor share all notes. C# Aeolian = E Ionian starting on C#.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think E major, emphasize C# as root. All notes work perfectly.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for understanding relative major/minor. Works universally.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Contains all four chord tones. Natural minor sound perfect for vi minor 7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: All notes work beautifully. Standard vim7 sound.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Essential for vim7 in E major. Perfect for E-B-C#m7-A progressions.

scaleCompatibility.rationale: Simple and effective. All E major notes work over C#m7.
scaleCompatibility.voiceLeadingTips: Think E major, emphasize C# as root. Maintains tonal clarity.
scaleCompatibility.recommendedUse: Perfect for diatonic vim7 approach. Works universally.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: The foundation of Western music in E major. Perfect for guitar - all open position chords.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Strong, optimistic, and guitar-friendly. The most natural guitar key.
commonProgressions.tips: E major is THE guitar key. All three chords are open position. Can enhance with E-A-B7.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-vi-IV
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop progression in E. The 'Axis of Awesome' progression.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Emotionally versatile. Works for entire songs.
famousSongs: Let It Be (transposed), With or Without You (transposed)
commonProgressions.tips: Extremely common in modern music. Can be played in any order.

commonProgressions.name: I-vi-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic '50s progression in E. The doo-wop changes.
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for retro vibes. Can enhance with Emaj7-C#m7-Amaj7-B7.

commonProgressions.name: ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: The fundamental jazz progression in E major.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated with strong resolution. The jazz standard.
commonProgressions.tips: Master this for jazz. Practice with extensions: F#m9-B13-Emaj9.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-vi-V
commonProgressions.description: Optimistic folk/pop progression with smooth voice leading.
famousSongs: Common in folk and country music
commonProgressions.tips: Perfect for acoustic guitar. Very singable.

commonProgressions.name: vi-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Modern pop/rock starting on vi minor in E.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Begins melancholic, builds to hopeful resolution.
commonProgressions.tips: Starting on vi creates immediate emotional impact.

commonProgressions.name: I-bVII-IV-I
commonProgressions.description: Modal rock progression in E borrowing bVII (D major).
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Bold, anthemic, powerful with modal flavor.
famousSongs: Sweet Child O' Mine (transposed), rock anthems
commonProgressions.tips: The D major is borrowed from E Mixolydian. Creates powerful rock sound.

commonProgressions.name: I-V-IV-V
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Energetic and driving. Double V creates urgency.
commonProgressions.tips: Great for high-energy sections. Repeated V emphasizes tension.

commonProgressions.name: I-IV-I-V
commonProgressions.description: Classic folk and country progression in E.
commonProgressions.tips: Very stable - keeps returning to I. Perfect for acoustic styles.

commonProgressions.name: vi-ii-V-I
commonProgressions.description: Extended jazz progression in E combining relative minor with ii-V-I.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: Sophisticated and smooth. Longer journey to resolution.
commonProgressions.tips: Extends ii-V-I with vi start. Great for learning harmony.

commonProgressions.name: I-iii-IV-V
commonProgressions.description: Ascending progression in E through scale degrees.
commonProgressions.tips: The iii chord (G#m) is underused but effective.

commonProgressions.name: 12-Bar Blues in E
commonProgressions.description: The classic 12-bar blues progression. E is the most common blues key.
commonProgressions.musicalCharacter: The quintessential blues sound. Raw, emotional, authentic.
famousSongs: Pride and Joy, Texas Flood, countless blues standards
commonProgressions.tips: E is THE blues key. Use E blues scale and E minor pentatonic for solos. Essential for any blues guitarist.
